intro: Pleurothallis freyi, also known as the Fringed Pleurothallis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis freyi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, flu, and other upper respiratory tract infections.
